threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for a polite smile on the bus—hard to find in Dalston. Instead, it's all about that intense stare out the window.
Last night, caught a guy on the 35 bus serenading a fellow passenger with an off-key rendition of 'Wonderwall'. The whole bus was in stitches.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Walking past the Turkish market, I realized I could name every spice as the old lady waved at me; that’s when I felt like it was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Kreuzberg's street art changes weekly, but the 'Kreuzberg monkey' graffiti remains untouched. It’s like a secret keeper of the neighborhood's vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate BART delays like it’s just another Tuesday. Outsiders are still standing there confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a Muni bus stop mid-route because a guy was practicing his sax solo on the sidewalk, crowd cheered. Classic SF vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always miss the quiet, tiny plazas in Gràcia. They crowd the main square, but the real charm is in the hidden corners with local cafes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones staring at the metro map like it's an ancient treasure map, trying to decipher the lines while missing the train.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’ve still got your Tesco bags—local knows you should be hitting the markets for your fruit and veg by now.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Clapham and overheard a guy trying to convince his mates that the bus takes the scenic route. It's just dark and quiet.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the kebab guy at my corner spot remembers my order and chats about the weather like we’re old friends. That’s when I knew.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a hidden club in an old factory where the toilets are art installations. It's wild and strangely charming.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can spot a tourist from a block away by their overly glossy map, but still help them find the best burrito.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a man serenading commuters at the Civic Center stop yesterday—full opera voice, no shame. Only in this city do you get that kind of entertainment on your way to work.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always assume the Gràcia Festa Major is just a party—it's really a labor of love for the locals, months of planning and creativity.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they still ask for a bottle of water in restaurants instead of just sipping from the tap. It's a little tell that makes me smile.
